summary: Backend-only validation completed for provider fallback, agent runner fallback integration, dependency-aware coordinator behavior, legacy coordinate compatibility, and docker-compose health/dependency rules. All targeted tests passed.
action_items: No blocking issues found for requested backend scope.
Optional hardening: split very large modules (proxy.py, backend/server.py) into smaller units for maintainability and safer review cycles.
Optional hardening: remove insecure/default env fallbacks for secrets and credentials to enforce fail-fast configuration in production-like environments.
critical_code_review_comments: proxy.py and backend/server.py are both very large (>1500 lines), which materially increases regression risk and slows safe change validation.
backend/server.py uses permissive defaults for several sensitive env vars (e.g., JWT secret/admin credentials), which can create unsafe startup configurations if env is misconfigured.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Added regression tests for /agent/coordinate (dependency-aware tasks, missing dependency blocking, and legacy workers path) and docker-compose integrity checks. JUnit report refreshed at /app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml.
rca of the issue: No regressions reproduced in requested scope. Primary risk now is architectural maintainability and configuration hardening, not functional correctness of fallback/coordination flows.
